name: Donald Trump Inaugurated as 47th President
summary: Donald Trump was inaugurated as the 47th President of the United States, returning to office after his 2020 defeat. His inauguration marked a significant shift in American domestic and foreign policy.

name: Los Angeles Wildfires
summary: A series of devastating wildfires broke out across the Los Angeles area, destroying thousands of structures and forcing mass evacuations. The fires were among the most destructive in California history.

name: Pope Francis Dies
summary: Pope Francis, the first Latin American pope, passed away at the age of 88. His pontificate was marked by efforts to modernize the Catholic Church and emphasize social justice.

name: Friedrich Merz Becomes German Chancellor
summary: Friedrich Merz and his CDU/CSU alliance won the German federal election, ending the Social Democrat-led coalition government. Merz became Chancellor amid growing concerns about the German economy and migration.

name: Ukraine-Russia Ceasefire Negotiations
summary: US-brokered ceasefire negotiations between Ukraine and Russia took place in Riyadh. The talks marked a significant diplomatic shift as the United States pushed for an end to the three-year conflict.

name: Syrian Reconstruction Begins
summary: Following the fall of Bashar al-Assad's government in late 2024, Syria's new transitional government began reconstruction efforts and opened diplomatic channels with Western nations for the first time in over a decade.

name: Operation Rising Lion
summary: Israel launched a large-scale military operation against Iran, targeting nuclear facilities and military infrastructure. The operation marked a significant escalation in the long-running shadow war between the two countries and triggered widespread regional tensions.
